reasoning: The Lean signature correctly encodes Axler Exercise 6.13. `[RCLike F]` captures "scalar field is ℝ or ℂ" (Axler's standing assumption); `[NormedAddCommGroup V] [InnerProductSpace F V]` is the standard Mathlib setup for a (positive-definite) inner product space. `e : Fin n → V` with `Orthonormal F e` represents the orthonormal list (e₁,...,eₙ). For RCLike F, `‖·‖` equals the absolute value/modulus, so `‖⟪v, e i⟫_F‖^2` is exactly `|⟨v,eᵢ⟩|²`, and the finite sum matches the informal RHS. `Submodule.span F (e '' Set.univ)` equals `Submodule.span F (Set.range e)`, which is the span of {e₁,...,eₙ}. The biconditional `↔` mirrors the "if and only if". Mathlib's conjugate convention doesn't affect absolute values. No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what is needed.
